steps.title: Identify the temporal frame
steps.content: "In a 2022 study" places the researchers' action at a specific moment in the past when the study occurred.

steps.title: Choose the completed past action
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: the sentence already has a main verb — analyzed ("researchers ... analyzed geotagged social media posts"). The blank cannot be another finite verb; it must be a participial phrase modifying researchers. Rule: when the main verb is elsewhere, a phrase modifying the subject must use a present or past participle, not a finite verb.
  • Choice A — "looking": present participle; "researchers, looking for a relationship..., analyzed posts" — correctly modifies "researchers" as a participial phrase. ✓
  • Choice B — "looked": simple past finite verb; creates two main verbs ("looked" and "analyzed") with no conjunction = run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — "were looking": past progressive finite verb; same double-verb problem as B. ✗
  • Choice D — "had looked": past perfect finite verb; again two main verbs with no conjunction. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
